title: installed-lifecycle-proposal
outOfScope: Applying updates to a runtime installation
Rollback, uninstall, purge, and release publication

sources.reason: Task-specific execution contract and acceptance evidence.
Approved lifecycle plan defining the three-way comparison and proposal requirements.
Installed manifest and digest fact model used as the current-project baseline.
Ownership and managed-region classification rules reused by proposal generation.
Read-only installed lifecycle status and release-evidence validation boundary.

riskAssessment.reason: Proposal generation is read-only, but incorrect ownership or digest classification could produce unsafe update advice.

executionDecision.reason: User authorized execution of the complete lifecycle plan; this Work Item is limited to read-only proposal generation.

preReviewWarnings: Review that proposal generation never writes Runtime files and fails closed on missing or inconsistent facts.

checkpointPolicy.reason: Record at least one checkpoint before finishing to reduce mid-task drift.

acceptance: The classifier emits every required category and preserves ownership, shared-region, generated, and historical distinctions.
The proposal records version, manifest hash, release evidence, safe/new/removed/conflict/shared changes, migration and rollback references, baseline/documentation impact, and resume conditions.
Proposal generation is read-only and does not modify Runtime or project files.
Unit tests cover equal-digest safe updates, project conflicts, shared changes, historical files, and new/removed template files.
CLI/Make and lifecycle documentation describe the proposal contract and read-only behavior.
guidelines: New public APIs must be documented.
Do not import internal modules from other features.

intent.problem: Installed lifecycle management needs a deterministic, reviewable comparison between the installed template release, a candidate release, and the current project so update decisions do not overwrite project-owned or modified content.
constraints: Use the installed manifest, ownership model, managed-region boundaries, and release evidence as inputs.
Fail closed when required facts are missing, malformed, or inconsistent.
Proposal generation must be read-only and must not write Runtime state.
Keep migration, rollback, baseline, and documentation effects explicit for later confirmation/apply work.
intent.rationale: A persisted proposal is the review boundary between facts and any future update apply operation.

rawUserRequest: 走完当前文档的流程；按计划逐个工单执行完整生命周期，包括 PR、合并、清理分支，直到全部工单完成。用户已授权所需确认范围。

declaredIntent.summary: Generate and validate a read-only installed lifecycle three-way update proposal.

scenarioCoverage.scenario: Installed digest equals current digest and candidate contains a safe template update
scenarioCoverage.reason: Scenario is explicitly covered by the approved plan and assigned focused test.

scenarioCoverage.scenario: Project-modified template file is classified as a conflict without overwrite
scenarioCoverage.reason: Scenario is explicitly covered by the approved plan and assigned focused test.

scenarioCoverage.scenario: Shared managed region changes are represented with explicit review impact
scenarioCoverage.reason: Scenario is explicitly covered by the approved plan and assigned focused test.

scenarioCoverage.scenario: Historical and generated files retain their special classifications
scenarioCoverage.reason: Scenario is explicitly covered by the approved plan and assigned focused test.

scenarioCoverage.scenario: Candidate adds and removes template files and records both in the proposal
scenarioCoverage.reason: Scenario is explicitly covered by the approved plan and assigned focused test.

scenarioCoverage.scenario: Missing or inconsistent manifest/release evidence fails closed without writes
scenarioCoverage.reason: Scenario is explicitly covered by the approved plan and assigned focused test.

restrictedWriteApproval.reason: User explicitly authorized the complete serial lifecycle plan, including Makefile and governance changes.

rollbackNote: Revert this Work Item diff and restore related tests and docs.
